message:
fix(fr-command): XCLAIM option-parser wording matches upstream [bd-frankenredis-rsls]

Multiple wording divergences in XCLAIM vs vendored redis 7.2.4
t_stream.c::xclaimCommand:

- min-idle-time non-numeric: fr 'value is not an integer or out
  of range' → upstream 'Invalid min-idle-time argument for
  XCLAIM' (also: upstream silently clamps negative to 0 instead
  of erroring).
- IDLE/TIME/RETRYCOUNT non-numeric or negative: fr generic →
  upstream 'Invalid <NAME> option argument for XCLAIM'.
- IDLE/RETRYCOUNT/LASTID without value (last token): fr WrongArity
  → upstream 'Unrecognized XCLAIM option <NAME>' (the && moreargs
  guard means the option itself isn't recognized when no value
  follows).
- Unknown option token: fr 'Invalid stream ID specified as stream
  command argument' (because the ID-parser fired first) → upstream
  'Unrecognized XCLAIM option <NAME>'.

Fix:
- ID-collection loop now breaks on the first unparseable token
  (mirrors upstream's NULL-client streamParseStrictIDOrReply); the
  unrecognised token surfaces via the option parser.
- Drop the empty-IDs-vector WrongArity check; let the option parser
  emit 'Unrecognized XCLAIM option …' for that case.
- Per-option errors thread the upstream wording.
- Silently clamp negative min-idle-time to 0.
